q-oni-oniart134-df53e657-8ab76ad8.stem: What minimum buoyancy must an individual lifesaving device have (general case)?
choices: At least 75 N
At least 750 N
At least 50 N
q-oni-oniart134-df53e657-8ab76ad8.explanation: ONI art. 134, al. 2: at least 75 N (except for vessels referred to in art. 134a).

q-oni-oniart134-df53e657-a940bc26.stem: What minimum buoyancy must individual lifesaving devices have according to ONI art. 134 (excluding the exception of art. 134a)?
choices: At least 75 N.
At least 100 N.
At least 50 N.
q-oni-oniart134-df53e657-a940bc26.explanation: ONI art. 134, al. 2 sets the buoyancy of individual devices at at least 75 N.

q-oni-oniart134-df53e657-f7855b82.stem: According to ONI art. 134, above which thresholds must a pleasure or sport boat be provided with an additional lifesaving device that can be thrown into the water?
choices: A power output greater than 30 kW, or a sail area exceeding 15 m2 for sailing boats.
A power output greater than 15 kW, or a sail area exceeding 30 m2.
A power output greater than 50 kW, or a sail area exceeding 10 m2.
q-oni-oniart134-df53e657-f7855b82.explanation: ONI art. 134, al. 5 applies to boats of more than 30 kW and to sailing boats whose sail area exceeds 15 m2.

q-oni-oniart143a-26df4c56-2fcf560c.stem: What does ONI art. 143a provide regarding the metacentric height of the loaded boat ready to depart?
choices: It must not be less than 1 m.
It must not be less than 0.5 m.
It must not exceed 1 m.
q-oni-oniart143a-26df4c56-2fcf560c.explanation: ONI art. 143a, al. 2 specifies that the metacentric height of the loaded boat ready to depart must not be less than 1 m.

q-oni-oniart143a-26df4c56-5e74c4ba.stem: For a loaded cargo boat ready to depart to be considered as having sufficient stability, what conditions does the text require?
choices: The heeling angle does not exceed 5 degrees and the side of the deck, at its lowest point, does not dip into the water.
The heeling angle does not exceed 15 degrees and the deck may dip up to 5 cm.
The heeling angle does not exceed 10 degrees, regardless of the position of the deck.
q-oni-oniart143a-26df4c56-5e74c4ba.explanation: According to ONI art. 143a, al. 2, the heeling angle must not exceed 5 degrees and the lowest side of the deck must not dip into the water.

q-oni-oniart144-82fa0c6a-11f7fd4c.stem: According to ONI art. 144, what is the prescribed freeboard for floating equipment?
choices: 90 cm in zone 2 and 45 cm in zone 3.
30 cm in zone 2 and 15 cm in zone 3.
100 cm in zone 2 and 50 cm in zone 3.
q-oni-oniart144-82fa0c6a-11f7fd4c.explanation: ONI art. 144, al. 5 sets the freeboard of floating equipment at 90 cm in zone 2 and 45 cm in zone 3.

q-oni-oniart144-82fa0c6a-92ee1624.stem: According to ONI art. 144, what is the freeboard of an undecked cargo boat in zone 2?
choices: 50 cm.
30 cm.
q-oni-oniart144-82fa0c6a-92ee1624.explanation: ONI art. 144, al. 2, let. b sets the freeboard of undecked boats at 100 cm in zone 2.

q-oni-oniart146-e6f12005-67f3713c.stem: According to ONI art. 146, with how many bulkheads must a cargo boat at least be fitted?
choices: At least one collision bulkhead and two bulkheads for the engine room.
At least three collision bulkheads at the bow.
At least one bulkhead for the engine room only.
q-oni-oniart146-e6f12005-67f3713c.explanation: ONI art. 146, al. 2 requires at least one collision bulkhead and two bulkheads for the engine room.

q-oni-oniart146-e6f12005-eca6cb88.stem: Which of these statements about the collision bulkhead is FALSE according to ONI art. 146?
choices: It may include doors and hatches if they are watertight.
It must be watertight and built from one side of the hull to the other.
It must be built from the bottom of the hold up to the deck.
q-oni-oniart146-e6f12005-eca6cb88.explanation: ONI art. 146, al. 5 prohibits any door, hatch or opening in the collision bulkhead.

q-oni-oniart148g-5963b92c-40c7d785.stem: Regarding the «CE» marking on sport boats placed on the market, what does ONI art. 148g say?
choices: The obligation to affix the «CE» marking is not applicable, but if it has already been affixed in accordance with the EU provisions, it may remain affixed.
The «CE» marking is mandatory for all sport boats placed on the market.
The «CE» marking must be removed if it was affixed in accordance with the EU provisions.
q-oni-oniart148g-5963b92c-40c7d785.explanation: ONI art. 148g, al. 2 provides that the «CE» marking is not mandatory but may remain affixed if it has already been affixed in accordance with the EU provisions.

q-oni-oniart148g-5963b92c-482a3efc.stem: In which language must the documentation relating to a sport boat be presented to the competent authority according to ONI art. 148g?
choices: In an official language of Switzerland or in English.
Exclusively in German.
Only in the manufacturer's language.
q-oni-oniart148g-5963b92c-482a3efc.explanation: ONI art. 148g, al. 6 requires that the documentation be presented in an official language of Switzerland or in English.

q-oni-oniart150-092b6842-b8583231.stem: ONI art. 150 allows the crew to be reduced by one deckhand, on boats of less than 350 tonnes and in good visibility. Which of these conditions is required?
choices: The travel time between the points of departure and arrival does not exceed 45 minutes.
The travel time between the points of departure and arrival does not exceed 90 minutes.
The boat must transport exclusively dangerous goods.
q-oni-oniart150-092b6842-b8583231.explanation: ONI art. 150, al. 4, let. b sets a maximum of 45 minutes of travel time allowing this reduction.

q-oni-oniart150-092b6842-e9e77154.stem: According to ONI art. 150, what is as a general rule the crew complement of a motorized cargo boat with a loading capacity greater than 1000 t?
choices: 2 deckhands.
1 deckhand.
3 deckhands.
q-oni-oniart150-092b6842-e9e77154.explanation: ONI art. 150, al. 2, let. a provides for 2 deckhands for motorized boats of more than 1000 t loading capacity.

q-oni-oniart163-1f9efaa2-1cd3c64f.stem: According to ONI art. 163, in which case is a construction number not necessary?
choices: For boats that are not built by professionals.
For all pleasure boats, without exception.
For priority passenger boats.
q-oni-oniart163-1f9efaa2-1cd3c64f.explanation: ONI art. 163, al. 1, let. g provides that a construction number is not necessary for boats not built by professionals.

q-oni-oniart163-1f9efaa2-baf912f2.stem: According to ONI art. 163, the granting of other derogations (not listed) requires in principle:
choices: The consent of the OFT.
The agreement of the Federal Council.
No additional authorization.
q-oni-oniart163-1f9efaa2-baf912f2.explanation: ONI art. 163, al. 3 provides that other derogations may only be granted with the consent of the OFT.

q-oni-oniart96a-83a6b5e5-5626de42.stem: What minimum third-party liability insurance coverage is required for the issuance of a collective navigation permit according to ONI art. 96a?
choices: Two million francs per accident.
One million francs per accident.
Five million francs per accident.
q-oni-oniart96a-83a6b5e5-5626de42.explanation: ONI art. 96a, al. 1, let. c requires a minimum coverage of two million francs per accident.

q-oni-oniart96a-83a6b5e5-9a9c82e3.stem: According to ONI art. 96a, in which case may a collective navigation permit be used?
choices: For breakdown-assistance and towing trips.
For regular commercial passenger transport.
For any private leisure trip of the owner.
q-oni-oniart96a-83a6b5e5-9a9c82e3.explanation: ONI art. 96a, al. 3, let. a authorizes the use of the collective permit for breakdown-assistance and towing trips.
